title: NYC Taxi Trips
summary: This project demonstrates the design and implementation of a modern data engineering pipeline using Microsoft Fabric. The pipeline ingests, transforms, and models real-world transportation data from the NYC Taxi & Limousine Commission, enabling structured analytics and interactive reporting.
problem: NYC Taxi & Limousine Commission data needed structured processing for analytics. Raw Parquet trip files and zone lookup CSV were ingested into a Fabric Lakehouse.
goal: Build an end-to-end data pipeline in Microsoft Fabric for ingestion, transformation, orchestration, and reporting.
approach:
Implemented a Lakehouse-to-Warehouse architecture in Microsoft Fabric:

- Ingested raw NYC TLC Parquet trip files and zone lookup CSV into the Lakehouse
- Used Data Warehouse for staging (stg.nyctaxi_yellow) and curated (dbo.nyctaxi_yellow) tables
- Orchestrated pipelines for ingestion, transformation, and enrichment via Dataflow Gen2
- Added stored procedures for metadata tracking, logging, and validation
- Built Power BI dashboards for trip analysis by volume, zones, and trends
results:
- Unified Fabric ecosystem for data engineering
- Automated pipelines with metadata tracking
- BI-ready datasets for trip analysis
- Scalable architecture for real-world data

gallery.alt: NYC Taxi Trips dashboard in Power BI showing trip volumes and zones

title: NYC Parking Violations
summary: A dbt project that models NYC parking violations with a medallion architecture (bronze -> silver -> gold). The pipeline cleans raw data, enriches it with fee logic, and delivers analytics-ready tables for reporting.
problem: Analysts need a consistent, documented way to study NYC parking violations across time, location, and code categories.
goal: Build a repeatable dbt pipeline that cleans, enriches, and models violations data for reporting.
approach: Stage raw tables in bronze, standardize and enrich records in silver, then publish aggregated gold metrics.
results: Analytics-ready tables with clear lineage from raw inputs to business metrics.

title: PhD Studenti ETL & Dashboards
summary: A fully containerized stack that ingests, transforms, enriches, and visualizes academic data to support data-driven monitoring of PhD student careers at Politecnico di Torino (DAUIN).
problem: The department lacked a structured, repeatable way to analyze PhD student careers across cycles, sources, and mobility programs.
goal: Enable transparent, AI-assisted enrichment and dashboarding so stakeholders can monitor activities, publications, and mobility over time.
approach: Containerized ingestion of CSV and PDF sources, AI-assisted extraction with Ollama, dbt-modeled warehouse and marts, and Grafana dashboards filtered by cycle, year, course, and mobility duration.
results: Department teams can run annual refreshes with curated files and instantly explore normalized data through Grafana dashboards.

metrics.label: Sources
metrics.description: Structured files plus AI-enriched unstructured PDFs.

metrics.label: Execution
metrics.description: All pipelines run in Docker for reproducible annual cycles.

metrics.label: Dashboards
metrics.description: Mobility, course, and activity views with cycle filters.

gallery.alt: Dashboard showing formation hours across PhD cycles and years.
Dashboard summarizing international mobility duration and participation.

title: Austin Trips
summary: This project implements an ETL pipeline to process historical bike sharing data from Austin (2013–present). The goal is to enable stakeholders to make informed decisions on bike usage patterns, station performance, demand distribution, and vehicle/subscription trends. The pipeline extracts raw trip data, transforms it to calculate useful metrics (e.g., trips per kiosk, trips per hour, top stations, subscription and bike type analysis), and loads the results into a format suitable for visualization and analysis.
problem: Operations teams were missing critical anomalies in a sea of logs, leading to costly downtime and pager fatigue.
goal: Deliver a streaming observability experience with clear alerting so responders see what matters within seconds.
approach: Unified disparate telemetry sources into a governed Kafka pipeline, layered dbt models for business-ready metrics, and delivered a realtime UI with progressive disclosure.
results: On-call staff now act on high-signal alerts with shared context in a single workspace, reducing triage loops and improving customer uptime.

gallery.alt: StreamPulse dashboards with live incident timeline.
Alert detail view highlighting correlated metrics.

title: Insulin Monitoring & Actuation
summary: An IoT microservice stack that publishes near-real-time glucose readings via MQTT, persists them in InfluxDB, and lets users review stats and trigger insulin actions through a Telegram bot.
problem: Manual glucose tracking and insulin logging lacked timeliness and remote visibility for caregivers.
goal: Deliver a containerized, publish–subscribe pipeline that captures glucose readings in near real time and enables controlled insulin actions through a chat interface.
approach: Implemented OOP Python sensor and actuator services publishing to MQTT, subscriber writes to InfluxDB, Telegram bot exposes history and commands, and a catalogue service registers clients/services in a microservice topology.
results: Achieved near-real-time persistence of glucose metrics, remote retrieval of trends via chat, and safe insulin actuation endpoints exposed over HTTP.

metrics.label: Ingestion pattern
metrics.description: Sensor publishers with MQTT; subscriber persists to InfluxDB.

metrics.label: Interfaces
metrics.description: Chatbot for history/commands; M2M for telemetry and control.

metrics.label: Architecture
metrics.description: Catalogue + sensor + actuator + subscriber services for scalability.
